params_echo.purpose: native SSE schema capture for T1c envelope redesign

findings.rendered_ai_response: annyeonghaseyo! mueoseul dowadeurilkkayo? (Hello! How can I help?)
event_types: message (11 rows) type label starts with 'm'
end (1 row) type label starts with 'e'
findings.schema_summary: Each 'message' event is a large JSON object with envelope fields: backend_uuid (matches thread slug), context_uuid, uuid, frontend_context_uuid, display_model ('turbo'), mode ('COPILOT' in first event, 'CONCISE' in subsequent), search_focus ('internet'), source, attachments, thread_url_slug, expect_search_results, gpt4, text_completed, blocks[], message_mode ('STREAMING'), answer_modes, reconnectable, cursor, search_mode ('SEARCH'), status ('PENDING' mid-stream), final_sse_message (false until last). Later events add classifier_results with widget_type, mhe_predictions (ML feature prob scores), domain_subdomain label. Final 'end' event body is literally '{}'.

mandatory_fields_status.raw_events: POPULATED (3 representative rows captured: index 1 initial, index 2 mid-stream with classifier, index 12 terminator)

implications_for_550_fail: 550 FAIL (thread-not-found toast) is now explainable. Perplexity's client parser requires very specific envelope fields (backend_uuid, context_uuid, uuid, frontend_context_uuid, thread_url_slug all UUIDs; blocks[] with specific intended_usage values; message_mode, status, final_sse_message). If APF's 875B envelope in services=127 lacks any of these or has wrong values, the client throws a 'thread not found' error because it cannot correlate the incoming envelope with the thread it just created. T1c fix: replace 875B envelope with a captured-verbatim envelope using THIS session's real UUIDs (substitution template) plus blocks=[{intended_usage: 'answer_tabs', diff_block: {...}}] with the warning text injected via patches. End event must be literal '{}'.
